title: Luffy Refuses to Bow to Buggy
description: On pages 20–23, this part raises the stakes in Orange Town as Buggy’s weapon and crew threaten the town directly.
scene: Orange Town becomes the stage for Nami’s schemes, Buggy’s violence, and Luffy’s refusal to act like a normal pirate.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: PHEW! I'm glad you found me!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: FIRST YOU FLY OFF WITH A BIRD, AND NOW YOU'RE PLAYING IN SOME CAGE...

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: HEY, DID HE JUST CALL THAT GUY Z-ZOLO?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: WHAT'S ZOLO THE PIRATE HUNTER DOING TALKING TO THAT THEF?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: THE CREWATE HE WAS TALKING ABOUT...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: THAT WAS ZOLO THE PIRATE HUNTER?

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: YOU MUST HAVE COME TO CAPTURE ME!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I HAVE NO INTEREST IN YOU.

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: WELL, I AM INTERESTED IN YOU. KILLING YOU WILL BE A NICE FEATHER IN MY CAP.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: BACK OFF BEFORE I PLUCK ALL YOUR FEATHERS.

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: GET'IM, CAP'N BUGGY! CUT'IM TO PIECES!!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: Give me all you've got, pirate Hunter, 'cause I'm about to get a look at your blood!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: > Over a thousand years ago, Viking raiders swept down from Scandinavia and ran amok through Europe. They came from the tries of Norway, Swe to why they were car easy; that's what they. Why did they call th know that, we'd have sand years and ask it

summary: The Orange Town conflict mixes Nami’s suspicious cleverness with Buggy’s theatrical cruelty. The section builds the unstable alliance that will oppose Buggy’s crew.
